facts.text: Pleasant Plains sits at the crossroads of South Middlebush Road and Claremont Road in Franklin Township, a spot locals call the heart of the CDP.
The area first appeared on maps as Pleasant Valley, settled by Dutch pioneers in 1702 when Staten Island landowners bought over 10,000 acres.
A 19th‑century general store and blacksmith shop, known as Laird's Brothers Hardware, still marks the historic core of Pleasant Plains.
The Delaware and Raritan Canal ran through Pleasant Plains, turning the township into a vital 19th‑century agricultural trade route until railroads took over.
In the late 1900s, preservationists rescued the one‑room Pleasant Plains School, moving it to the municipal complex on DeMott Lane for future restoration.
A 1998 voter referendum created an Open Space Preservation Plan that keeps thousands of acres of farmland, including the rural landscapes near Pleasant Plains, from non‑farm development.
